sections.paragraph:
pl. נֵרֹות (from the root נוּר to shine).

(1) a lamp, a candle, Zep 1:12 often used of the lamps of the holy candlestick (הַמְּנֹורָה), Exo 25:37 35:14 37:23 39:37 once of the candlestick itself, 1Sa 3:3. It is figuratively applied in various ways-(a) to happiness (compare אֹור letter e), however the idea of a light is still retained, e.g. Pro 13:9, נֵר רְשָׁעִים יִדְעָךְ “the lamp of the wicked shall be put out,” i.e. they grope in darkness; Pro 20:20 31:18 Psa 18:29, “thou wilt light my lamp,” i.e. cause my affairs to prosper; Job 29:3.-(b) to glory. So 2Sa 21:17, where David is called the lamp of Israel. (The same figure is used in Syriac and Arabic, e.g. see Barhebræus, Assemanni, ii. p. 266.)-The proper signification is retained Pro 20:27, “the lamp of the Lord (lighted by him) is the soul of a man who explores the recesses of the breast.”

(2) [Ner], pr.n. of the grandfather of Saul, 1Sa 14:50, 51 1Sa 14:51 26:5 1Ch 8:33.
